pattern: For each ordered pair of letters, a Kadane-style scan treating one as +1 and the other as -1 (while ensuring at least one of the negative letter is used) finds the maximum count difference.
hints: Fix two specific letters, a major and a minor. Within a substring the variance for this pair is (count of major) minus (count of minor).
Map major to +1 and minor to -1 and you want the maximum subarray sum, but the substring must contain at least one minor letter to be valid.
For each of the 26x26 ordered pairs run Kadane, resetting when the running sum goes negative, and only count a candidate once a minor letter has appeared in the current window.

edge_inputs.description: two letters with a clear best window
all distinct letters give zero variance
single character cannot form a nonzero variance
all same character still needs two distinct letters so zero
minor letter at the ends must still be counted
best pair is not the most frequent letter

stress.description: moderate string over a small alphabet so many pairs are viable
stress.note: small alphabet keeps the brute force O(n^2 * 26) tractable while stressing Kadane
